narrative: OMX30 enters the session technically well-positioned (bull regime, +8.4% above 200-MA, MACD bullish) but faces headwinds from deteriorating global sentiment and weak ADX (9.5), which dampens conviction on directional moves. European peers sold off overnight (DAX -0.02%, CAC -0.29%, FTSE -0.69%), and negative correlation signal (-0.227%) suggests decoupling is unlikely. Geopolitical risks (US-Iran escalation, trade rhetoric) have lifted VIX to 16.68 and are driving a subtle risk-off. USD/SEK strength (+0.21%) is constructive for Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson, but sentiment compression in tech/commodities limits upside. Watch for early weakness into US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET.
morningBrief: OMX30 faces a cautious open. While technicals remain constructively bullied (above 200-MA, MACD positive, RSI 53 neutral), weak ADX (9.5) signals low trend conviction—apply 30% discount to magnitude. European weakness (FTSE -0.69%) and negative global correlation (-0.227%) set a tepid tone. Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran) and rising VIX (16.68, +2.39%) are driving subtle risk-off rotation. USD/SEK +0.21% lifts exporter names (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson), but NASDAQ flat and gold weakness (-0.53%) constrain upside. Key levels: support 3085 (lower Bollinger), resistance 3180 (50-MA). Watch for capitulation into US jobs data at 14:30 CET; any break below 3100 could trigger momentum reversal. Sector call: Industrials/Exports outperform, Tech/Defensive underperform.
drivers: Weak ADX (9.5) reduces trend conviction—reduce magnitude by 30%
Negative correlation signal (-0.227%) despite OMX bull regime, risk-off tone
Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran tensions) creates asymmetric downside risk
Strong USD/SEK (+0.21%) supports exporters but offset by global risk sentiment

sectorImpacts.reason: USD/SEK +0.21% improves export competitiveness; however weak ADX limits conviction.
US 10Y yield fell -0.27%; NIM headwind outweighs any stability from risk-off positioning.
NASDAQ flat, global risk-off sentiment, VIX elevated (+2.39%). DCF pressure from yield volatility.
Gold -0.53% (headwind), Copper +0.60% (modest tailwind); conflicting signals, net neutral.
Risk-off environment favours defensive rotation; geopolitical uncertainty supports haven demand.

watchList.reason: USD/SEK strength (+0.21%) supports export pricing; strong industrial play with ADX caveat.
Defensive bid into geopolitical risk-off; pharma should outperform on haven rotation.
Tech sentiment weak (NASDAQ flat, global risk-off), VIX elevated. Sentiment compression into jobless claims.
EUR/SEK flat, but consumer discretionary vulnerable in risk-off; global demand headwinds.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (initial & continuing) at 14:30 CET is primary focus. No major Swedish data. Watch for Trump commentary on Iran/trade—potential for vol spike.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran) could trigger sharper risk-off and break below 3100 support; VIX spike would pressure equities and compress valuations, especially in rates-sensitive and discretionary sectors.
technicalSummary: Bull regime intact but ADX 9.5 (weak) = low conviction, apply -30% to magnitude
RSI 53.4 neutral, Stochastic K 64.2 / D 73.5 (no extremes)
Bollinger %B 0.69 (upper half but not overbought); width 6.38% (normal)
52-week: 89% of range, -2.8% from high—near-term fatigue risk
No RSI extreme; no mean reversion flag
